title: Divorcing Money from Monetary Policy

source.name: Federal Reserve Bank of New York

summary: The Federal Reserve Bank of New York released a press release on August 6, 2008 announcing a forthcoming article in the Economic Policy Review series by Todd Keister, Antoine Martin, and James McAndrews. The research proposes that paying interest on reserve balances at the target interest rate can decouple the relationship between the quantity of reserves and the interest rate target, creating a "floor system" for monetary policy implementation.
key_points: Central banks have shifted primary focus from money supply measures to short-term interest rates, but the quantity of money and interest rate policy remain fundamentally linked
Paying interest on reserve balances at the target rate can eliminate tensions between monetary policy and money supply objectives
This mechanism allows a central bank to set the quantity of reserves and interest rate target independently of one another
During acute market stress, this allows an increase in reserve supply without driving market interest rates below the target
The floor system allows central banks to set reserve supply according to payment or liquidity needs while encouraging efficient resource allocation
Authors note policymakers should evaluate potential operational challenges and effects on financial markets
why_it_matters: This paper anticipated the Fed's later adoption of interest on reserves (implemented October 2008) as a policy tool. The floor system concept allows central banks to conduct large-scale reserve provision without losing control of the interest rate target—critical for understanding modern monetary policy operations and the Fed's response to the 2008 financial crisis. This is a historically significant record of early research that directly informed post-crisis policy implementation.
macro_context: The research addresses the relationship between monetary aggregates and interest rate policy—a core macroeconomics topic. This is historical academic research from 2008 that proved prescient; the theoretical framework was implemented by the Fed within months of publication.
market_structure_context: This research directly addresses market structure: the mechanics of monetary policy implementation, the relationship between reserve supply and interest rate targets, and the design of the "floor system" for reserve management. These are core central bank market operations and plumbing concepts that became operational reality post-2008.
